moves.note: Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero
Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet the mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Reduce CRWD to 10% to manage single-name concentration.
Increase QQQI to 25% for greater income and stability.
Buy TSLA at 5% as a high-score quality name.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation and reduces overall risk.
conservative.read: This conservative plan aims for a modest ~1.5%/month outlook, focusing on capital preservation. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, leading to lower expected drawdowns. However, potential upside is also limited.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to align with concentration limits.
Sell SOXL to exit a flagged leveraged ETF position.
Reduce SPAL to 2% to lower overall leveraged exposure.
Trim CRWD to 10% to manage single-name concentration.
Buy TSLA at 8% and AVGO at 5% to add quality tech setups.
This plan balances growth with risk management, reducing overall leverage.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets ~2.5%/month, focusing on risk-first growth. It reduces leverage and concentration while adding quality tech names. Expect moderate drawdowns with potential for solid returns.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to comply with concentration limits.
Sell SOXL to exit the flagged, downtrending leveraged ETF.
Maintain SPAL at 2% for some leveraged exposure.
Add to CRWD, increasing to 15%, as it is a working high-score name.
Buy TSLA at 10% and NVDU at 8% as top-scoring long setups.
This plan leans into growth with calculated leverage and concentration.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4%/month by stretching growth and using leverage. It carries significantly higher risk and potential for larger drawdowns, especially given leveraged ETF decay. Strict stop discipline is crucial.

moves.note: Reduce broad index for income focus
Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero
Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and enhance stability.
Increase QQQI to 35% to significantly boost income generation.
Reduce QQQ to 10% to shift focus towards income.
Trim SMH to 5% and CRWD to 5% to reduce sector and high-beta exposure.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk.
This plan prioritizes steady monthly distributions and low drawdown.
income.read: This income plan aims for ~1%/month, prioritizing steady distributions and capital preservation. It significantly reduces growth-oriented and leveraged exposure. While focusing on income, NAV can still fall in selloffs, and distributions are not guaranteed.
